mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents through standardized communication protocols. It simplifies the deployment complexity and enhances security for agent-based applications, making it ideal for production environments. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes and specific resource requirements.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to a directory in your PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using Google Cloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.30

```

> This command creates a cluster named 'kagenti-cluster' with 3 nodes in the specified zone.
commands: gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.30

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the new Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file with the credentials for the new cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the list of nodes with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Install Kagenti
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti application on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Adds the Kagenti Helm repository.
> Installs the Kagenti application in the 'kagenti' namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up additional configurations for autoscaling and monitoring.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Applies the autoscaling configuration.
> Applies the monitoring configuration.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description: Be aware that running a Kubernetes cluster incurs costs based on the resources used.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti application and warns about potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> This command removes the Kagenti release from your cluster.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it, leading to data loss.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Backup your current configuration, upgrade the application, and verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Backs up the current configuration.
> Upgrades the Kagenti application to the specified version.
> Verifies that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the server: dial tcp 35.185.XXX.XXX:443: i/o timeout'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster is not reachable, possibly due to network issues.

**Fix:**
Check your internet connection and ensure that the cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: release kagenti not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kagenti release has not been installed in the specified namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure that you have installed Kagenti using the correct namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: failed to install CRDs'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) are not installed properly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/crds.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may have configuration issues or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kagenti' for more details.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new agent communication protocols and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kagenti' showing all pods in 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti
gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.30
gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
